title: where guards on transitions
manager: `where` clauses attach a boolean guard to a transition, so an edge only fires when its condition holds — the workhorse of every data-dependent state machine. It reads as "take this arrow where balance > 0," making the guard part of the transition's identity, and it composes with the resolution order so a guarded and an unguarded edge on the same action have a defined, non-surprising precedence. This is the first place the expression language reaches out and touches the graph itself.
cs: `where <expr>` guard on transitions; the expression is a total boolean (W6.34); participates in edge resolution order (W6.89); a false guard makes the edge ineligible, not an error.
